_note: Relocation decision-support data. The `official` block per metro carries CITED datapoints — BEA Regional Price Parities (cost), HUD 2BR Fair Market Rents (housing), BLS metro unemployment (jobs), and the principal VA Medical Center — each with source URL and vintage. The 1–5 tiers remain illustrative comparison aids derived for the prototype; verify all factors independently and check facilities at va.gov/find-locations.

tierGuide.colTier: Cost of living: 1 = lowest cost, 5 = highest (illustrative).
tierGuide.community: Veteran presence: 1–5, higher = larger/denser veteran community (illustrative).
tierGuide.safetyTier: Sample comfort tier: 1–5, higher = more favorable overall picture — always varies street by street; visit in person.
tierGuide.businessTier: Small-business climate: 1–5, higher = friendlier taxes/regulatory feel (illustrative).

metros.name: San Antonio

va.note: Major VA medical center presence plus a large military medical community.

metros.colNote: Below-average costs for a big metro.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: many starter homes listed in the $200k–$300k range.

jobs.note: Military-adjacent employers actively recruit veterans across healthcare, cyber, IT, and federal work.

metros.schoolsNote: Many districts across the metro; quality varies by suburb — tour schools the way you'd tour houses.
metros.communityNote: Known as 'Military City USA' — one of the largest veteran communities in the country.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax; active small-business and franchise scene.
metros.notes: A frequent default pick for transitioning veterans — for good reason.

vamc.name: Audie L. Murphy Memorial Veterans' Hospital (VA South Texas Health Care System)

official.notes: San Antonio-New Braunfels, TX MSA / HUD Metro FMR Area. Unemployment cross-checked against BLS Southwest regional release (4.1% May 2026).

metros.name: Dallas–Fort Worth

va.note: VA medical center plus a broad clinic network across the metroplex.

metros.colNote: Moderate overall; costs vary widely by suburb.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: roughly $250k–$400k for many family homes depending on the suburb.

jobs.note: One of the largest, most diverse job markets in the country — nearly every track has depth here.

metros.schoolsNote: Suburb-by-suburb differences are large; many families pick the suburb by the school district first.
metros.communityNote: Big veteran population spread across a very large metro — find your pocket.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax; steady corporate relocations keep hiring strong.
metros.notes: Scale is the superpower; commute time is the tradeoff.

vamc.name: Dallas VA Medical Center (VA North Texas Health Care System)

official.notes: RPP and unemployment are for the full Dallas-Fort Worth-Arlington, TX MSA. HUD splits the metroplex: Dallas HMFA 2BR = $1,931 (shown); Fort Worth-Arlington HMFA 2BR = $1,723 (same HUD file). System also includes Garland VA Medical Center and Fort Worth outpatient clinics.

metros.name: Tampa

va.note: Major VA medical center presence.

metros.colNote: Costs have climbed but remain mid-pack for a coastal big metro.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: many homes in the $300k–$400k range; flood insurance can add real cost near water.

jobs.note: Defense-adjacent employers and a strong veteran-hiring base.

metros.schoolsNote: Mix of district and choice options; families often shortlist two or three zones before renting.
metros.communityNote: Large veteran and military-retiree community anchored by nearby commands.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax; busy service-business market.
metros.notes: Hurricane preparedness and insurance costs are part of the deal — budget for both.

vamc.name: James A. Haley Veterans' Hospital (VA Tampa Health Care System)

official.notes: Tampa-St. Petersburg-Clearwater, FL MSA.

metros.name: Jacksonville

va.note: Large VA outpatient clinic presence in town; the region's full VA medical center is a drive away.

metros.colNote: One of the more affordable big coastal metros.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $250k–$350k common for family homes.

jobs.note: Port, logistics, and ship-repair work plus a big Navy footprint.

metros.schoolsNote: Very large county district plus charter/magnet options — research zones early.
metros.communityNote: Major Navy town — active-duty and veteran families are everywhere.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax; logistics and trades stay busy.
metros.notes: A budget-friendlier Florida option if beach access matters more than nightlife.

vamc.name: No VAMC in the metro — outpatient clinics only (Jacksonville 1, Jacksonville 2, Jacksonville North VA Clinics). Nearest full VA medical center: Malcom Randall VAMC, Gainesville (VA North Florida/South Georgia Health Care System)

official.notes: Jacksonville, FL MSA / HUD Metro FMR Area. Confirms the dataset's CBOC-only characterization: Jacksonville hosts multi-specialty VA outpatient clinics; inpatient VAMCs for the system are in Gainesville and Lake City, FL.

metros.name: Phoenix

va.note: VA medical center plus a growing clinic network across the Valley.

metros.colNote: Moderate; newer outer suburbs stretch dollars further.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $300k–$400k common; new-build suburbs run cheaper.

jobs.note: Trades demand runs hot year-round — construction and HVAC rarely slow down.

metros.schoolsNote: Many districts and open-enrollment options; quality varies widely across the Valley.
metros.communityNote: Large veteran population spread across a sprawling metro.
metros.businessNote: Growth-friendly climate for trades and service businesses.
metros.notes: Summer heat is a lifestyle decision — visit in July before you commit.

vamc.name: Carl T. Hayden Veterans' Administration Medical Center (VA Phoenix Health Care System)

official.notes: Phoenix-Mesa-Chandler, AZ MSA.

metros.name: Atlanta

va.note: VA medical center presence plus metro clinics.

metros.colNote: Moderate for a big metro; intown vs. suburb changes the math a lot.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: wide range — roughly $275k–$425k for many family homes by area.

jobs.note: Corporate headquarters depth plus one of the busiest logistics hubs anywhere.

metros.schoolsNote: District quality varies sharply by county — school-first house hunting is the local norm.
metros.communityNote: Solid veteran presence with several installations within day-trip range.
metros.businessNote: Big customer base for service businesses and franchises.
metros.notes: Traffic shapes daily life here — pick your side of town before you pick the job.

vamc.name: Joseph Maxwell Cleland Atlanta VA Medical Center (Decatur, GA — VA Atlanta Health Care System)

official.notes: Atlanta-Sandy Springs-Roswell, GA MSA / HMFA.

metros.name: Nashville

va.note: VA medical center in the metro.

metros.colNote: Moderate but rising; outlying counties are cheaper.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $325k–$450k common near the core; less further out.

jobs.note: A healthcare-industry capital with a booming trades and small-business scene.

metros.schoolsNote: Metro and surrounding-county districts differ a lot; many families settle one county out.
metros.communityNote: Growing veteran community; a major Army post is about an hour northwest.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax; strong small-business energy.
metros.notes: Growth cuts both ways — opportunity is up, and so are prices.

vamc.name: Nashville VA Medical Center (VA Tennessee Valley Healthcare System)

official.notes: Nashville-Davidson--Murfreesboro--Franklin, TN MSA / HMFA. System also includes Alvin C. York VA Medical Center in Murfreesboro, inside the same metro.

metros.name: San Diego

va.note: Major VA medical center presence plus a dense military medical network.

metros.colNote: One of the most expensive metros on this list.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: many family homes well above $700k; renting first is common.

jobs.note: Defense, biotech, and aviation employers prize clearances and military maintenance experience.

metros.schoolsNote: Generally solid options across the county, but housing cost decides which zones are realistic.
metros.communityNote: Huge Navy and Marine Corps presence — the veteran network is deep and active.
metros.businessNote: Higher taxes and regulatory load — build both into any business plan.
metros.notes: Weather and community are elite; cost is the tradeoff to price honestly.

vamc.name: Jennifer Moreno Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center (La Jolla — VA San Diego Healthcare System)

official.notes: San Diego-Chula Vista-Carlsbad, CA MSA.

metros.name: Denver

va.note: Full VA medical center in the metro.

metros.colNote: Above-average costs, driven mostly by housing.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $450k+ is common for family homes.

jobs.note: Tech, aerospace-adjacent, and corporate work with an outdoor-lifestyle draw.

metros.schoolsNote: Strong variation between districts; many families target specific suburbs.
metros.communityNote: Solid veteran presence with military communities along the Front Range.
metros.businessNote: Healthy market, moderate costs and regulation.
metros.notes: The mountains are the point — housing cost is the toll booth.

vamc.name: Rocky Mountain Regional VA Medical Center (Aurora, CO — VA Eastern Colorado Health Care System)

official.notes: Denver-Aurora-Centennial, CO MSA.

metros.name: Seattle–Tacoma

va.note: VA medical center presence at multiple sites around the Sound.

metros.colNote: High near Seattle; the Tacoma side runs meaningfully cheaper.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $500k+ common closer in; south-Sound suburbs run lower.

jobs.note: Tech, aerospace, and the port keep both office and trades work strong.

metros.schoolsNote: District quality varies around the Sound; south-end value zones are popular with military families.
metros.communityNote: A large joint base south of Tacoma keeps the veteran network strong.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax, but business taxes and costs are real — model both.
metros.notes: Gray winters are real; the summers make the case.

vamc.name: Seattle VA Medical Center (VA Puget Sound Health Care System; second campus: American Lake VA Medical Center, Tacoma)

official.notes: RPP and unemployment are for the Seattle-Tacoma-Bellevue, WA MSA. HUD splits the metro: Seattle-Bellevue HMFA 2BR = $2,501 (shown); Tacoma HMFA 2BR = $1,971 (same HUD file). American Lake VAMC page: https://www.va.gov/puget-sound-health-care/locations/american-lake-va-medical-center/

metros.name: Washington, DC metro

va.note: VA medical center plus dense clinic coverage across the region.

metros.colNote: Expensive across DC, Maryland, and Northern Virginia; distance buys affordability plus a commute.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: close-in family homes commonly $600k+; far suburbs less.

jobs.note: The center of gravity for federal hiring, cleared work, and government contracting.

metros.schoolsNote: Ranges from excellent to struggling within short distances — zone research is essential.
metros.communityNote: Dense veteran and military population across the whole region.
metros.businessNote: Unmatched GovCon opportunity; general operating costs are high.
metros.notes: If federal work or SDVOSB contracting is the plan, this is the deep end of the pool.

fmr2br.areaName: Washington-Arlington-Alexandria, DC-VA-MD HUD Metro FMR Area

unemployment.note: From BLS Mid-Atlantic MSA table snippet (labor force 3,436,200 / unemployed 133,600, not seasonally adjusted). DC-MD Metropolitan Division alone was 5.2% in May 2026 per DC DOES release citing BLS (https://does.dc.gov/release/washington-metro%E2%80%99s-unemployment-rate-52-percent-0). bls.gov resisted direct fetch.

vamc.name: Washington VA Medical Center (VA Washington DC health care)

official.notes: Full-service VAMC at 50 Irving St NW. Federal workforce contraction in 2025-26 pushed the DC-MD division unemployment rate up (~5.2%); the wider MSA rate remains lower (3.9%).

metros.name: Virginia Beach–Norfolk

va.note: Full VA medical center in the region plus clinics.

metros.colNote: Moderate for a coastal metro.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $300k–$400k common for family homes.

jobs.note: Shipyards, naval aviation, and defense IT anchor the market.

metros.schoolsNote: Several city districts side by side; military families trade notes constantly — use that network.
metros.communityNote: One of the densest military and veteran regions in the country.
metros.businessNote: Steady defense-adjacent demand for service businesses.
metros.notes: If you want to stay close to the fleet and the culture, this is it.

fmr2br.areaName: Virginia Beach-Norfolk-Newport News, VA-NC HUD Metro FMR Area

unemployment.note: Value via USAFacts snippet of BLS LAUS data (https://usafacts.org/answers/what-is-the-unemployment-rate/metro-area/virginia-beach-va/), April 2026; bls.gov resisted automated fetch. Regional context: rate rose to ~4.1% in Nov 2025 on federal job cuts before easing.

vamc.name: Hampton VA Medical Center (VA Hampton health care)

official.notes: Hampton VAMC (100 Emancipation Dr, Hampton) is a 432-bed teaching facility serving eastern VA and northeastern NC — full VAMC access for the metro.

metros.name: Minneapolis–St. Paul

va.note: Full VA medical center in the metro.

metros.colNote: Moderate; strong wages help the math.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $300k–$375k common for family homes.

jobs.note: Fortune 500 density plus a major health sector — steady corporate and clinical hiring.

metros.schoolsNote: Generally well-regarded metro districts with real variation — compare a few suburbs.

metros.businessNote: Strong corporate base; taxes run higher.
metros.notes: Winter is a commitment — pay and quality of life balance it for many.

fmr2br.areaName: Minneapolis-St. Paul-Bloomington, MN-WI HUD Metro FMR Area

unemployment.note: Value via search snippet of BLS LAUS data as republished by FRED series MINN427URN (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/MINN427URN); bls.gov and FRED resisted automated fetch.

vamc.name: Minneapolis VA Medical Center (Minneapolis VA Health Care System)

official.notes: Full VAMC (1 Veterans Dr, Minneapolis) with 24/7 emergency department; VISN 23 flagship affiliated with the University of Minnesota.

metros.name: Colorado Springs

va.note: VA clinics in town plus military treatment facilities; the full VA medical center is about an hour north.

metros.colNote: Moderate-high for a mid-size metro; cheaper than Denver.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $350k–$450k common.

jobs.note: Defense and space-adjacent employers hire cleared talent constantly.

metros.schoolsNote: Multiple districts with distinct personalities; military families cluster around the well-known ones.
metros.communityNote: Multiple installations make this one of the most veteran-dense mid-size metros anywhere.
metros.businessNote: Defense-heavy economy supports service businesses.
metros.notes: Outdoors lifestyle plus a huge military community — the classic post-service landing zone.

vamc.name: No VAMC in the metro — outpatient clinic only: PFC Floyd K. Lindstrom Department of Veterans Affairs Clinic (VA Eastern Colorado Health Care System). Nearest full VA medical center: Rocky Mountain Regional VAMC, Aurora (Denver metro), roughly an hour north

official.notes: Colorado Springs, CO MSA / HUD Metro FMR Area. Confirms the dataset's 'nearby' VA characterization: large multi-specialty clinic in town (3141 Centennial Blvd) with same-day Veterans Express Care; inpatient care is at Rocky Mountain Regional VAMC in Aurora.

metros.name: Raleigh–Durham

va.note: Full VA medical center in the metro (Durham side).

metros.colNote: Moderate and rising with the region's growth.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $325k–$425k common.

jobs.note: Universities and the tech corridor create steady analyst, IT, and education pipelines.

metros.schoolsNote: Well-regarded overall with fast-growing suburbs — capacity and zoning shift; check the current map.
metros.communityNote: Moderate veteran presence with strong veteran pipelines at area employers.
metros.businessNote: Growth market with a reputation for being easy to do business in.
metros.notes: Education-and-tech corridor energy at mid-size-metro scale.

vamc.name: Durham VA Medical Center (VA Durham Health Care System) — 508 Fulton St, Durham; 251-bed tertiary care and teaching hospital. Raleigh side is served by CBOCs (Raleigh VA Clinic and others) under the same system

official.notes: BEA/BLS/HUD all treat Raleigh-Cary, NC and Durham-Chapel Hill, NC as separate metros. Values shown are Raleigh-Cary MSA. Durham-Chapel Hill from same sources: RPP 97.572 (2024); 2BR FMR $1,711 (FY2026, Durham-Chapel Hill HMFA); unemployment 3.2% May 2026 (LAUMT372050000000003).

metros.name: Pittsburgh

va.note: Multiple VA medical facilities in the metro — strong VA presence.

metros.colNote: Genuinely affordable for a metro with this much infrastructure.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: plenty of solid homes under $250k.

jobs.note: A real trades-and-hospitals economy — steady work without coastal costs.

metros.schoolsNote: City and suburban districts differ widely; many neighborhoods punch above their price.
metros.communityNote: Established veteran community with deep service-organization roots.
metros.businessNote: Low overhead for service businesses and trades shops.
metros.notes: One of the strongest affordability-plus-VA-access combos on this list.

vamc.name: Pittsburgh VA Medical Center-University Drive (VA Pittsburgh Healthcare System; second medical center campus: H. John Heinz III, O'Hara Township)

official.notes: Pittsburgh, PA MSA / HUD Metro FMR Area. VA Pittsburgh operates two medical centers plus six CBOCs in the region — matches the dataset's 'multiple VA medical facilities' note.

metros.name: Huntsville

va.note: VA clinic coverage in town; the region's full VA medical center is a significant drive away.

metros.colNote: Low costs relative to the salaries the defense sector pays here.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $250k–$325k common.

jobs.note: Defense and aerospace contracting hub — one of the best SDVOSB launch pads anywhere.

metros.schoolsNote: Several well-regarded suburban districts; growth is testing capacity — verify current zoning.
metros.communityNote: Dense veteran and defense-civilian community around the arsenal.
metros.businessNote: GovCon opportunity per capita is hard to beat.
metros.notes: Small city, outsized federal-contracting economy.

vamc.name: No VAMC in the metro — outpatient clinics only (Huntsville VA Clinic, Huntsville West VA Clinic, Huntsville South VA specialty clinic; VA Birmingham Health Care System). Nearest full VA medical center: Birmingham VA Medical Center, roughly 100 miles south

official.notes: Huntsville, AL MSA. Confirms the dataset's CBOC characterization; Huntsville South VA specialty clinic (cardiology, chemo infusion, podiatry, neurology) opened Feb 2026, expanding local coverage.

metros.name: Boise

va.note: Full VA medical center in town.

metros.colNote: Moderate after a fast run-up; still cheaper than the coasts.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $375k–$450k common.

jobs.note: Population growth keeps trades and service businesses booked out.

metros.schoolsNote: Generally solid districts; fast-growing suburbs are adding schools — check what's actually open.
metros.communityNote: Moderate but tight-knit veteran community.
metros.businessNote: Growth-friendly, low-friction environment for small business.
metros.notes: Outdoors life plus a real VAMC — a rare mid-size combination.

vamc.name: Boise VA Medical Center (VA Boise Health Care System) — 500 W Fort St, Boise

official.notes: Boise City, ID MSA / HUD Metro FMR Area.

metros.name: Oklahoma City

va.note: Full VA medical center in the metro.

metros.colNote: Among the lowest big-city costs in the country.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: many family homes $180k–$275k.

jobs.note: A major aviation-maintenance workforce plus logistics, energy, and federal work.

metros.schoolsNote: Suburban districts are the usual family pick; the metro core varies more.
metros.communityNote: A large installation keeps the veteran and federal-civilian network active.
metros.businessNote: Low overhead makes first businesses easier to launch.
metros.notes: Your dollar goes far here — and aviation maintenance is a hometown industry.

vamc.name: Oklahoma City VA Medical Center (VA Oklahoma City Health Care System) — 921 NE 13th St, Oklahoma City

official.notes: Oklahoma City, OK MSA / HUD Metro FMR Area. System also runs 15 outpatient clinics including several within the metro (North OKC, South OKC, Norman, Yukon, Tinker AFB).

metros.name: Tucson

va.note: Full VA medical center in town.

metros.colNote: Noticeably cheaper than Phoenix.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $250k–$325k common.

jobs.note: Aerospace and defense presence with a lower cost base than the bigger desert metros.

metros.schoolsNote: District and charter options vary across the valley; families compare a handful of zones.
metros.communityNote: Air Force presence and retiree community keep veteran networks strong.
metros.businessNote: Modest costs; steady demand for trades and services.
metros.notes: Desert living at a calmer pace and price than Phoenix.

vamc.name: Tucson VA Medical Center (VA Southern Arizona Health Care System) — 3601 S 6th Ave, Tucson

official.notes: Tucson, AZ MSA. System includes 7 CBOCs across southern Arizona plus Northwest and Southeast Tucson VA clinics inside the metro.

metros.name: Columbus

va.note: Major VA outpatient/ambulatory-care center in town; the nearest full inpatient VA medical center is a drive away.

metros.colNote: Affordable for the size and job base.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $250k–$325k common.

jobs.note: Diverse Midwest economy — logistics, insurance, tech, and a huge university.

metros.schoolsNote: Ring suburbs carry strong district reputations; the city core varies more.
metros.communityNote: Moderate veteran presence with active student-veteran and employer programs.
metros.businessNote: Frequent test market for franchises — a telling sign for small business.
metros.notes: Quietly one of the most balanced value picks in the Midwest.

rpp.note: Columbus, OH MSA, RPPs: All items (2023 = 94.630). Official BEA MARPP dataset.

fmr2br.note: Columbus, OH HUD Metro FMR Area, 2BR. Official HUD FY26 file (FY2025 was $1,445).

unemployment.note: BLS LAUS, Columbus OH MSA, not seasonally adjusted. April 2026 = 2.8, March 2026 = 3.4. BLS reported Columbus had the largest YoY metro rate decline in spring 2026.

vamc.name: Chalmers P. Wylie Veterans Outpatient Clinic (VA Central Ohio health care)
vamc.note: CBOC/ambulatory-care center only — Columbus has no full inpatient VA medical center in-metro; nearest full VAMCs (e.g., Dayton, Chillicothe) are a drive away. Matches the 'CBOC' level in relocationMetros.json.

metros.name: Fayetteville

va.note: Full VA medical center in town serving a large military region.

metros.colNote: Very affordable — the installation economy keeps prices grounded.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: many homes under $250k.

jobs.note: The installation economy hires steadily; service businesses serve a constant military population.

metros.schoolsNote: County district plus on-post options; military families share current intel freely — ask.
metros.communityNote: Life revolves around one of the largest Army posts — veteran networks are everywhere.
metros.businessNote: A reliable customer base for veteran-owned service businesses.
metros.notes: If you want to stay inside the military ecosystem, this is home turf.

rpp.note: Fayetteville, NC MSA, RPPs: All items (2023 = 91.854). Official BEA MARPP dataset.

fmr2br.note: Fayetteville, NC HUD Metro FMR Area, 2BR. Official HUD FY26 file (FY2025 was $1,240).

unemployment.note: BLS LAUS, Fayetteville NC MSA, not seasonally adjusted. April 2026 = 4.3, March 2026 = 4.4.

vamc.name: Fayetteville VA Medical Center (VA Fayetteville Coastal health care)
vamc.note: Full VA medical center (Complexity Level 1c, 2300 Ramsey St), ~10 miles from Fort Bragg/Fort Liberty; system serves ~90,000 veterans across 19 counties.

metros.name: Spokane

va.note: Full VA medical center in town.

metros.colNote: A far lower cost base than the coastal side of the state.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $300k–$375k common.

jobs.note: Regional health hub with steady trades demand.

metros.schoolsNote: City and valley districts each have loyal fans; tour both sides before choosing.
metros.communityNote: Air Force base nearby and an established veteran community.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax; small-city overhead.
metros.notes: Four seasons, real VA access, and coastal-Washington escape pricing.

vamc.name: Mann-Grandstaff Department of Veterans Affairs Medical Center (VA Spokane Health Care System) — 4815 N Assembly St, Spokane

official.notes: Spokane-Spokane Valley, WA MSA; HUD Spokane, WA HUD Metro FMR Area.

metros.name: Knoxville

va.note: VA clinic coverage in town; the regional VA medical center is roughly a 1.5-hour drive.

metros.colNote: Low costs at the foot of the Smokies.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $275k–$350k common.

jobs.note: Steady trades market, growing population, low overhead for a first business.

metros.schoolsNote: County districts vary by zone; several suburbs are known family magnets.
metros.communityNote: Moderate veteran presence with strong community feel.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax; friendly costs for trades shops.

vamc.name: No VAMC in the metro — outpatient care only: William C. Tallent Department of Veterans Affairs Outpatient Clinic, Knoxville (VA Mountain Home Health Care System), plus Knox County VA Clinic. Nearest full VA medical center: James H. metros.name: Sioux Falls

va.note: A full VA medical center in a small city — a rare combination.

metros.colNote: Low costs with surprisingly strong local wages.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $250k–$325k common.

metros.schoolsNote: A manageable set of districts; class sizes and access are small-city advantages.
metros.communityNote: Modest veteran population but a tight one — the VAMC is a natural hub.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax; consistently low-friction for small business.

metros.name: Fargo

va.note: Full VA medical center in town.

metros.colNote: Very low costs; employers compete for reliable people.
metros.housingSample: Sample scan: many homes under $275k.

metros.schoolsNote: Well-regarded small-city districts with easy access.
metros.communityNote: Smaller veteran community, anchored by the VAMC and Guard presence.
metros.businessNote: Low overhead and loyal local customer bases.
metros.notes: The winter is the entrance exam. Pass it and the math works.

vamc.name: Fargo VA Medical Center (VA Fargo Health Care System) — 2101 Elm St N, Fargo

metros.name: Cheyenne

va.note: Full VA medical center in town.

metros.housingSample: Sample scan: $275k–$350k common.

metros.schoolsNote: One county district — simple to evaluate; small class sizes.
metros.communityNote: The base plus a small population means veterans are a visible part of town life.
metros.businessNote: No state income tax and famously light regulatory load.
metros.notes: High plains, low costs, full VA access — wind included at no extra charge.

va.note: VA clinic in town; the full VA medical center is about an hour away in Nashville.

metros.notes: Military-town familiarity at small-town prices, with a big city within reach.

vamc.name: No VAMC in the metro — outpatient clinics only: Clarksville VA Clinic, Montgomery County VA Clinic, Dalton Drive VA Dental Clinic, and Fort Campbell VA Clinic (VA Tennessee Valley Healthcare System). Nearest full VA medical center: Nashville VA Medical Center, roughly an hour southeast

official.notes: Clarksville, TN-KY MSA / HUD Metro FMR Area. Confirms the dataset's CBOC characterization; VA has five clinic sites serving Clarksville veterans, and a new multi-specialty Clarksville VA clinic is slated to open by 2030.

metros.name: Rural + telehealth pattern

va.note: Anchor to a VA community clinic (CBOC), lean on VA telehealth, and confirm Community Care coverage before committing.

metros.colNote: The lowest-cost pattern of all — land and housing prices most metros can't touch.
metros.housingSample: Sample pattern: acreage plus a modest home for what a metro starter house costs.

jobs.note: Work that travels well: driving, trades, and service businesses — and remote work changes the math entirely.

metros.schoolsNote: Small districts vary widely — visit the actual school; small class sizes are the common upside.
metros.communityNote: Fewer veterans nearby by definition — plan deliberate connection: VSO posts, online groups, regular trips to town.
metros.businessNote: Low overhead and little competition — but a smaller customer base; validate demand first.
metros.notes: Not a place — a pattern. It works when you anchor healthcare and community on purpose, before the move.
